verses.commentary: Spiritual death preceded spiritual life. Transgressions and sins are not weakness but death. The unregenerate are corpses, incapable of moving toward God without His intervention.
The world's ways and Satan's influence govern the spiritually dead. This age and its ruler dominate those outside Christ. Disobedience is not merely moral failure but demonic bondage.
All humans, Jews and Gentiles, lived in fleshly desires and were by nature children of wrath. Universal sinfulness means universal condemnation. Nature, not just choices, makes humanity guilty.
God's great love and rich mercy interrupt humanity's trajectory toward judgment. Divine love initiates rescue when humans are dead in sin. Mercy is God's disposition toward the guilty.
God made us alive with Christ while we were dead. Union with Christ in His resurrection is the source of spiritual life. Grace alone saves; the parenthetical reminder cannot be overstated.
Believers are raised and seated with Christ in heavenly realms. Past tense indicates accomplished reality. Christians already participate in Christ's exaltation, though not yet fully experienced.
Future ages will display God's grace's incomparable riches. Kindness to us in Christ demonstrates divine character eternally. Salvation is God's eternal self-revelation.
Grace through faith saves, not human origin or effort. Faith itself is God's gift, preventing any boasting. Salvation from start to finish is divine work, not human achievement.
Works do not save, eliminating all boasting. If salvation depended on works, pride would be justified. Grace excludes merit; boasting is forever ruled out.
Believers are God's workmanship, created in Christ for good works. God prepared works beforehand for us to walk in. Good works are result, not cause, of salvation.
Gentiles were excluded from covenant blessings, bearing the label 'uncircumcised' from those trusting in physical ritual. Ethnic and religious identity separated them from Israel's privileges.
Before Christ, Gentiles were alienated from Messiah, excluded from Israel, strangers to covenant promises, without hope or God. Their condition was utter spiritual destitution.
In Christ, those far away are brought near by His blood. Proximity to God is purchased by Christ's sacrifice. Distance is eliminated through atonement.
Christ Himself is peace between Jews and Gentiles, making both one by destroying the dividing wall. Unity is not negotiated but created by Christ's work. He is peace personified.
Christ abolished the law's commands and regulations, creating one new humanity. The law divided; Christ unites. Peace comes through creating a new people, not reforming old categories.

Through the cross, Christ reconciles both Jews and Gentiles to God in one body. The cross kills hostility between groups and between humanity and God. Vertical and horizontal peace are achieved together.
Christ preached peace to those far (Gentiles) and near (Jews). The gospel message is peace with God and each other. Proclamation makes reconciliation effective.
Through Christ, both groups have access to the Father by one Spirit. Trinity cooperates in granting believers entry to God. Access is trinitarian: through Christ, by the Spirit, to the Father.
Gentiles are no longer foreigners but fellow citizens and household members. Status changes from outsiders to family. Belonging to God's people and family replaces exclusion.

The church is built on the apostles and prophets' foundation, with Christ as the cornerstone. Foundation establishes structure; cornerstone aligns and holds it together. Christ is essential, not peripheral.
The whole building is joined together in Christ, rising into a holy temple. Growth is organic and unified. The church corporately becomes God's dwelling place.
Believers are being built together into God's dwelling by the Spirit. Present tense indicates ongoing process. God inhabits His people through the Spirit's presence.
